preamble: Fortition and Delateralization involve the strengthening of a lateral liquid into a stop consonant. This often occurs in initial positions or as part of a general process of consonant hardening.

examples.note: Logudorese Sardinian development showing a fortition stage where [l] strengthens towards a dental stop.
Historical stages in Na-Dene languages often show alternations between lateral liquids and dental stops.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
